# 1. Compilation Errors

## 1.1 Cannot Find Symbol: Method

**Severity:** 🔴 **CRITICAL**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** Very High (40% of AI-generated plugins)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/commands/TeleportCommand.java:[23,28] cannot find symbol
  symbol:   method getPlayer(java.lang.String)
  location: class org.bukkit.Bukkit
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when using deprecated or version-specific methods  
**What the user sees:** Build fails, JAR not created

### Root Cause
AI models trained on older Bukkit/Spigot documentation (pre-1.12) often generate code using deprecated methods that were removed in modern versions. The `getPlayer(String)` method exists but has case-sensitivity issues; AI should use `getPlayerExact(String)` or UUID-based lookups.

**Common deprecated methods AI generates:**
- `getPlayer(String)` → Should use `getPlayerExact(String)` or `getPlayer(UUID)`
- `setHealth(int)` → Should use `setHealth(double)`
- `getOfflinePlayer(String)` → Should use `getOfflinePlayer(UUID)` for reliability
- `Player.sendMessage(String)` → Paper prefers Adventure Components
- `ChatColor` → Should use MiniMessage or Adventure Component API

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.commands;

import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.command.Command;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandExecutor;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandSender;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;

public class TeleportCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(CommandSender sender, Command command, String label, String[] args) {
        if (args.length == 0) {
            sender.sendMessage("Usage: /tp <player>");
            return true;
        }
        
        // ❌ WRONG: getPlayer() has issues with partial matches
        Player target = Bukkit.getPlayer(args[0]);
        
        if (target == null) {
            sender.sendMessage("Player not found!");
            return true;
        }
        
        if (sender instanceof Player) {
            Player player = (Player) sender;
            player.teleport(target.getLocation());
        }
        
        return true;
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.commands;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.command.Command;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandExecutor;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandSender;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;

public class TeleportCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(@NotNull CommandSender sender, @NotNull Command command, 
                           @NotNull String label, @NotNull String[] args) {
        if (!(sender instanceof Player player)) {
            sender.sendMessage(Component.text("Only players can use this command!", NamedTextColor.RED));
            return true;
        }
        
        if (args.length == 0) {
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("Usage: /tp <player>", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
            return true;
        }
        
        // ✅ CORRECT: Use getPlayerExact for exact name matching
        Player target = Bukkit.getPlayerExact(args[0]);
        
        if (target == null) {
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("Player not found or offline!", NamedTextColor.RED));
            return true;
        }
        
        player.teleport(target.getLocation());
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("Teleported to " + target.getName(), NamedTextColor.GREEN));
        
        return true;
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use Paper 1.21.4 API with Adventure Components. Never use deprecated Bukkit methods. 
Use getPlayerExact() for name lookups and prefer UUID-based player references."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] No `ChatColor` usage (use Adventure Components)
- [ ] No `getPlayer(String)` without exact match
- [ ] All CommandExecutor methods use `@NotNull` annotations
- [ ] Pattern matching for instanceof (Java 16+)

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create a `PlayerUtils` class that wraps all player lookup logic:
```java
public class PlayerUtils {
    public static Optional<Player> getOnlinePlayer(String name) {
        return Optional.ofNullable(Bukkit.getPlayerExact(name));
    }
    
    public static Optional<Player> getOnlinePlayer(UUID uuid) {
        return Optional.ofNullable(Bukkit.getPlayer(uuid));
    }
}
```

## 1.2 Package Does Not Exist

**Severity:** 🔴 **CRITICAL**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** High (25% of new projects)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/MyPlugin.java:[3,8] package org.bukkit does not exist
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/MyPlugin.java:[4,8] package org.bukkit.plugin.java does not exist
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time, immediately after project creation  
**What the user sees:** Every single Java file fails to compile

### Root Cause
AI forgets to add Paper/Spigot dependency to `pom.xml` or `build.gradle`, or uses wrong repository URL. This is the #1 error in fresh AI-generated projects.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```xml
<!-- pom.xml - Missing Paper dependency -->
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 
         http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>kannada.kotila.aanna</groupId>
    <artifactId>MyPlugin</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <properties>
        <java.version>21</java.version>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
    </properties>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.11.0</version>
                <configuration>
                    <source>21</source>
                    <target>21</target>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
    
    <!-- ❌ MISSING: repositories and dependencies sections -->
</project>
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```xml
<!-- pom.xml - Complete with Paper dependency -->
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 
         http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>kannada.kotila.aanna</groupId>
    <artifactId>MyPlugin</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <properties>
        <java.version>21</java.version>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
    </properties>

    <!-- ✅ REQUIRED: Paper repository -->
    <repositories>
        <repository>
            <id>papermc</id>
            <url>https://repo.papermc.io/repository/maven-public/</url>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <!-- ✅ REQUIRED: Paper dependency with PROVIDED scope -->
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.papermc.paper</groupId>
            <artifactId>paper-api</artifactId>
            <version>1.21.4-R0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <finalName>${project.artifactId}-${project.version}</finalName>
        <resources>
            <resource>
                <directory>src/main/resources</directory>
                <filtering>true</filtering>
            </resource>
        </resources>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.11.0</version>
                <configuration>
                    <source>21</source>
                    <target>21</target>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Create a complete Maven project with Paper 1.21.4 dependency (provided scope), 
correct repository configuration, and resource filtering enabled."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] `<repositories>` section includes Paper repository
- [ ] Paper dependency has `<scope>provided</scope>`
- [ ] Resource filtering enabled for plugin.yml variable substitution
- [ ] Correct Paper version (check https://repo.papermc.io/)

**Architecture Prevention:**
Always start new projects with a template `pom.xml`. Store in team wiki or Git template repo.

## 1.3 Class/Interface Expected

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** Medium (15% of plugins with custom configs)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/resources/plugin.yml:[1,1] class, interface, or enum expected
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when plugin.yml is in wrong location or has syntax errors  
**What the user sees:** Confusing error pointing to YAML file in Java compilation

### Root Cause
Maven compiler tries to compile `plugin.yml` as Java source code because:
1. File is placed in `src/main/java/` instead of `src/main/resources/`
2. Resources directory not configured in `pom.xml`
3. YAML syntax error interpreted as Java code

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```
Project Structure:
MyPlugin/
├── src/
│   └── main/
│       └── java/
│           └── kannada/kotila/aanna/
│               ├── MyPlugin.java
│               └── plugin.yml          ❌ WRONG LOCATION
```

```yaml
# plugin.yml with syntax error
name: MyPlugin
version: 1.0.0
main: kannada.kotila.aanna.MyPlugin
api-version: 1.16
commands:
  test:
    description: Test command
    usage: /test
    permission: myplugin.test
  reload:                                # ❌ Missing colon, invalid YAML
    description Reload plugin            # ❌ WRONG: Missing colon after 'description'
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```
Project Structure:
MyPlugin/
├── src/
│   └── main/
│       ├── java/
│       │   └── kannada/kotila/aanna/
│       │       └── MyPlugin.java
│       └── resources/                   ✅ CORRECT LOCATION
│           └── plugin.yml
```

```yaml
# plugin.yml - Correct syntax
name: MyPlugin
version: '${project.version}'            # ✅ Resource filtering variable
main: kannada.kotila.aanna.MyPlugin
api-version: '1.16'
author: KotilaDev
description: A comprehensive plugin
website: https://example.com

commands:
  test:
    description: Test command
    usage: /<command>
    permission: myplugin.test
    permission-message: "You don't have permission!"
  reload:
    description: Reload plugin configuration  # ✅ CORRECT: Colon present
    usage: /<command> reload
    permission: myplugin.admin

permissions:
  myplugin.test:
    description: Allows use of test command
    default: true
  myplugin.admin:
    description: Administrative permissions
    default: op
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Place plugin.yml in src/main/resources/ (not src/main/java/). 
Use YAML syntax validation. Enable Maven resource filtering."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] `plugin.yml` is in `src/main/resources/`
- [ ] All YAML keys have colons (`:`)
- [ ] String values with special chars use quotes
- [ ] Version uses `${project.version}` for auto-sync
- [ ] Valid `api-version` specified

**Architecture Prevention:**
Configure Maven to validate YAML during build:
```xml
<build>
    <resources>
        <resource>
            <directory>src/main/resources</directory>
            <filtering>true</filtering>
        </resource>
    </resources>
</build>
```

## 1.4 Illegal Start of Expression

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** Medium (10% of complex commands)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/commands/EconomyCommand.java:[45,13] illegal start of expression
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/commands/EconomyCommand.java:[45,20] ';' expected
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time with syntax errors in switch statements, lambda expressions, or malformed method calls  
**What the user sees:** Cryptic error that doesn't clearly indicate the syntax mistake

### Root Cause
AI generates invalid Java syntax, often in:
1. Switch expressions (Java 14+) with wrong syntax
2. Lambda expressions with missing parentheses
3. Method references with incorrect context
4. Annotation placement errors

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.commands;

import org.bukkit.command.Command;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandExecutor;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandSender;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;

public class EconomyCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(CommandSender sender, Command command, String label, String[] args) {
        if (!(sender instanceof Player player)) {
            sender.sendMessage("Only players!");
            return true;
        }
        
        if (args.length == 0) {
            player.sendMessage("Usage: /economy <balance|pay|set>");
            return true;
        }
        
        // ❌ WRONG: Invalid switch expression syntax
        String result = switch (args[0].toLowerCase()) {
            case "balance" -> {
                // Missing yield statement
                getBalance(player);  // ❌ Returns void but switch expects String
            }
            case "pay" -> {
                if (args.length < 3) {
                    return "Usage: /economy pay <player> <amount>";  // ❌ return in switch
                }
                processPay(player, args[1], args[2]);
            }
            case "set" -> {
                // ❌ Multiple statements without yield
                checkAdmin(player);
                setBalance(args[1], args[2]);
            }
            default -> "Unknown subcommand";  // ✅ This part is correct
        };
        
        player.sendMessage(result);
        return true;
    }
    
    private void getBalance(Player player) {
        // Implementation
    }
    
    private void processPay(Player player, String target, String amount) {
        // Implementation
    }
    
    private void checkAdmin(Player player) {
        // Implementation
    }
    
    private void setBalance(String player, String amount) {
        // Implementation
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.commands;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import org.bukkit.command.Command;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandExecutor;
import org.bukkit.command.CommandSender;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;

public class EconomyCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    
    private final EconomyManager economyManager;
    
    public EconomyCommand(EconomyManager economyManager) {
        this.economyManager = economyManager;
    }
    
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(@NotNull CommandSender sender, @NotNull Command command, 
                           @NotNull String label, @NotNull String[] args) {
        if (!(sender instanceof Player player)) {
            sender.sendMessage(Component.text("Only players can use this command!", NamedTextColor.RED));
            return true;
        }
        
        if (args.length == 0) {
            sendUsage(player);
            return true;
        }
        
        // ✅ CORRECT: Proper switch expression with yield
        Component result = switch (args[0].toLowerCase()) {
            case "balance" -> {
                double balance = economyManager.getBalance(player.getUniqueId());
                yield Component.text("Your balance: $" + balance, NamedTextColor.GREEN);
            }
            case "pay" -> {
                if (args.length < 3) {
                    yield Component.text("Usage: /economy pay <player> <amount>", NamedTextColor.RED);
                }
                yield processPay(player, args[1], args[2]);
            }
            case "set" -> {
                if (!player.hasPermission("economy.admin")) {
                    yield Component.text("No permission!", NamedTextColor.RED);
                }
                if (args.length < 3) {
                    yield Component.text("Usage: /economy set <player> <amount>", NamedTextColor.RED);
                }
                yield setBalance(player, args[1], args[2]);
            }
            default -> Component.text("Unknown subcommand! Use: balance, pay, set", NamedTextColor.RED);
        };
        
        player.sendMessage(result);
        return true;
    }
    
    private void sendUsage(Player player) {
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("Economy Commands:", NamedTextColor.GOLD));
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("  /economy balance - Check your balance", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("  /economy pay <player> <amount> - Pay another player", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("  /economy set <player> <amount> - Set balance (Admin)", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
    }
    
    private Component processPay(Player sender, String targetName, String amountStr) {
        // Proper implementation with validation
        try {
            double amount = Double.parseDouble(amountStr);
            if (amount <= 0) {
                return Component.text("Amount must be positive!", NamedTextColor.RED);
            }
            
            Player target = sender.getServer().getPlayerExact(targetName);
            if (target == null) {
                return Component.text("Player not found!", NamedTextColor.RED);
            }
            
            if (economyManager.transfer(sender.getUniqueId(), target.getUniqueId(), amount)) {
                target.sendMessage(Component.text("You received $" + amount + " from " + sender.getName(), NamedTextColor.GREEN));
                return Component.text("Sent $" + amount + " to " + target.getName(), NamedTextColor.GREEN);
            } else {
                return Component.text("Insufficient funds!", NamedTextColor.RED);
            }
        } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
            return Component.text("Invalid amount!", NamedTextColor.RED);
        }
    }
    
    private Component setBalance(Player admin, String targetName, String amountStr) {
        // Implementation
        try {
            double amount = Double.parseDouble(amountStr);
            if (amount < 0) {
                return Component.text("Amount cannot be negative!", NamedTextColor.RED);
            }
            
            Player target = admin.getServer().getPlayerExact(targetName);
            if (target == null) {
                return Component.text("Player not found!", NamedTextColor.RED);
            }
            
            economyManager.setBalance(target.getUniqueId(), amount);
            return Component.text("Set " + target.getName() + "'s balance to $" + amount, NamedTextColor.GREEN);
        } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
            return Component.text("Invalid amount!", NamedTextColor.RED);
        }
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use Java 21 switch expressions with proper yield statements for multi-line cases. 
All switch arms must return the same type. Use Component for messages."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] Switch expressions use `yield` for multi-statement cases
- [ ] All switch arms return compatible types
- [ ] No `return` statements inside switch expressions
- [ ] Lambda expressions have proper parameter syntax `(param) -> {}`

**Architecture Prevention:**
Use command routing pattern instead of complex switch expressions:
```java
public class EconomyCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    private final Map<String, SubCommand> subCommands = new HashMap<>();
    
    public EconomyCommand(EconomyManager manager) {
        subCommands.put("balance", new BalanceCommand(manager));
        subCommands.put("pay", new PayCommand(manager));
        subCommands.put("set", new SetCommand(manager));
    }
    
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(CommandSender sender, Command command, String label, String[] args) {
        if (args.length == 0) {
            sendUsage(sender);
            return true;
        }
        
        SubCommand subCommand = subCommands.get(args[0].toLowerCase());
        if (subCommand == null) {
            sender.sendMessage(Component.text("Unknown subcommand!", NamedTextColor.RED));
            return true;
        }
        
        subCommand.execute(sender, Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 1, args.length));
        return true;
    }
}
```

## 1.5 Method Does Not Override

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** Medium (12% of listener implementations)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/listeners/JoinListener.java:[12,5] method does not override or implement a method from a supertype
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when `@Override` annotation is used on incorrect method signature  
**What the user sees:** Event handler not executing, no errors at runtime

### Root Cause
AI generates event handlers with wrong method signatures:
1. Wrong parameter type (e.g., `PlayerJoinEvent` vs `PlayerLoginEvent`)
2. Missing `@EventHandler` annotation
3. Method name typo
4. Wrong access modifier (private instead of public)
5. Static method when instance method expected

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners;

import org.bukkit.event.EventHandler;
import org.bukkit.event.Listener;
import org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerJoinEvent;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;

public class JoinListener implements Listener {
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Method signature doesn't match any interface method
    @Override
    public void onPlayerJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {  // ❌ Wrong method name
        Player player = event.getPlayer();
        player.sendMessage("Welcome!");
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Private method won't be called
    @EventHandler
    private void onJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        // This will never execute
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Static method
    @EventHandler
    public static void handleJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        // This will cause errors
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Missing @EventHandler
    @Override
    public void onPlayerQuit(PlayerQuitEvent event) {
        // Won't execute without @EventHandler
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import org.bukkit.event.EventHandler;
import org.bukkit.event.EventPriority;
import org.bukkit.event.Listener;
import org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerJoinEvent;
import org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerQuitEvent;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;

public class JoinListener implements Listener {
    
    private final PlayerDataManager dataManager;
    
    public JoinListener(PlayerDataManager dataManager) {
        this.dataManager = dataManager;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Public, non-static, with @EventHandler
    @EventHandler(priority = EventPriority.HIGH)
    public void onPlayerJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        Player player = event.getPlayer();
        
        // Load player data asynchronously
        dataManager.loadPlayerData(player.getUniqueId()).thenAccept(data -> {
            Component welcomeMessage = Component.text("Welcome back, ", NamedTextColor.GREEN)
                .append(Component.text(player.getName(), NamedTextColor.GOLD))
                .append(Component.text("!", NamedTextColor.GREEN));
            
            player.sendMessage(welcomeMessage);
        });
        
        // Set custom join message
        event.joinMessage(Component.text(">>> ", NamedTextColor.GRAY)
            .append(Component.text(player.getName(), NamedTextColor.YELLOW))
            .append(Component.text(" joined the server", NamedTextColor.GRAY)));
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Proper quit handler
    @EventHandler(priority = EventPriority.MONITOR)
    public void onPlayerQuit(PlayerQuitEvent event) {
        Player player = event.getPlayer();
        
        // Save player data asynchronously
        dataManager.savePlayerData(player.getUniqueId());
        
        // Set custom quit message
        event.quitMessage(Component.text("<<< ", NamedTextColor.GRAY)
            .append(Component.text(player.getName(), NamedTextColor.YELLOW))
            .append(Component.text(" left the server", NamedTextColor.GRAY)));
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Cancelled event handling
    @EventHandler(priority = EventPriority.LOW, ignoreCancelled = true)
    public void onPlayerJoinSecurity(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        Player player = event.getPlayer();
        
        // Security check - only executes if event not cancelled by other plugins
        if (dataManager.isBanned(player.getUniqueId())) {
            player.kick(Component.text("You are banned!", NamedTextColor.RED));
        }
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Create event listeners with public methods, @EventHandler annotation, 
correct event type parameters. Never use @Override on event handlers unless 
implementing a custom interface."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] Event handlers are `public` (not private/protected)
- [ ] Event handlers are NOT `static`
- [ ] `@EventHandler` annotation present
- [ ] Correct event type parameter (e.g., `PlayerJoinEvent`, not `PlayerEvent`)
- [ ] No `@Override` annotation unless implementing custom interface
- [ ] Listener registered in `onEnable()`

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create abstract base listener class:
```java
public abstract class BaseListener implements Listener {
    protected final JavaPlugin plugin;
    
    protected BaseListener(JavaPlugin plugin) {
        this.plugin = plugin;
        plugin.getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(this, plugin);
    }
    
    protected void runAsync(Runnable task) {
        plugin.getServer().getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, task);
    }
    
    protected void runSync(Runnable task) {
        plugin.getServer().getScheduler().runTask(plugin, task);
    }
}
```

### Related Errors
- [2.4 IllegalStateException (Async)](#24-illegalstateexception-async)
- [3.2 Event Cancellation Confusion](#32-event-cancellation-confusion)

## 1.6 Incompatible Types

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** High (20% of config-heavy plugins)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/managers/ConfigManager.java:[34,25] incompatible types: java.lang.Object cannot be converted to java.lang.String
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/managers/ConfigManager.java:[45,20] incompatible types: int cannot be converted to java.lang.String
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when type casting or conversion is incorrect  
**What the user sees:** Build fails with type mismatch errors

### Root Cause
AI doesn't properly handle YAML configuration value types. `config.get()` returns `Object`, requiring explicit casting or type-safe getters.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.managers;

import org.bukkit.configuration.file.FileConfiguration;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;

public class ConfigManager {
    private final JavaPlugin plugin;
    private FileConfiguration config;
    
    public ConfigManager(JavaPlugin plugin) {
        this.plugin = plugin;
        loadConfig();
    }
    
    public void loadConfig() {
        plugin.saveDefaultConfig();
        plugin.reloadConfig();
        config = plugin.getConfig();
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: get() returns Object, not String
    public String getPrefix() {
        return config.get("prefix");  // ❌ Incompatible types
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Trying to assign int to String
    public String getMaxPlayers() {
        return config.getInt("max-players");  // ❌ int cannot be converted to String
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: No null check or default value
    public int getTimeout() {
        return config.getInt("timeout");  // Returns 0 if missing, causing bugs
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Unsafe cast
    public List<String> getAllowedCommands() {
        return (List<String>) config.get("allowed-commands");  // ❌ Unchecked cast warning
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.managers;

import org.bukkit.configuration.file.FileConfiguration;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

public class ConfigManager {
    private final JavaPlugin plugin;
    private FileConfiguration config;
    
    // Default values as constants
    private static final String DEFAULT_PREFIX = "&7[&bMyPlugin&7] ";
    private static final int DEFAULT_MAX_PLAYERS = 100;
    private static final int DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 30;
    
    public ConfigManager(JavaPlugin plugin) {
        this.plugin = plugin;
        loadConfig();
    }
    
    public void loadConfig() {
        plugin.saveDefaultConfig();
        plugin.reloadConfig();
        config = plugin.getConfig();
        validateConfig();
    }
    
    private void validateConfig() {
        boolean needsSave = false;
        
        if (!config.contains("prefix")) {
            config.set("prefix", DEFAULT_PREFIX);
            needsSave = true;
        }
        
        if (!config.contains("max-players")) {
            config.set("max-players", DEFAULT_MAX_PLAYERS);
            needsSave = true;
        }
        
        if (!config.contains("timeout")) {
            config.set("timeout", DEFAULT_TIMEOUT);
            needsSave = true;
        }
        
        if (needsSave) {
            plugin.saveConfig();
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Use type-specific getter with default
    @NotNull
    public String getPrefix() {
        return config.getString("prefix", DEFAULT_PREFIX);
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Return int, not String
    public int getMaxPlayers() {
        return config.getInt("max-players", DEFAULT_MAX_PLAYERS);
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Explicit default value
    public int getTimeout() {
        int timeout = config.getInt("timeout", DEFAULT_TIMEOUT);
        return timeout > 0 ? timeout : DEFAULT_TIMEOUT;  // Additional validation
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Safe list retrieval with validation
    @NotNull
    public List<String> getAllowedCommands() {
        List<String> commands = config.getStringList("allowed-commands");
        return commands != null ? new ArrayList<>(commands) : new ArrayList<>();
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Boolean with default
    public boolean isDebugMode() {
        return config.getBoolean("debug", false);
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Double with validation
    public double getMultiplier() {
        double multiplier = config.getDouble("multiplier", 1.0);
        if (multiplier < 0.1 || multiplier > 10.0) {
            plugin.getLogger().warning("Multiplier out of range (0.1-10.0), using default 1.0");
            return 1.0;
        }
        return multiplier;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Enum with fallback
    public GameMode getDefaultGameMode() {
        String modeStr = config.getString("default-gamemode", "SURVIVAL");
        try {
            return GameMode.valueOf(modeStr.toUpperCase());
        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
            plugin.getLogger().warning("Invalid game mode: " + modeStr + ", using SURVIVAL");
            return GameMode.SURVIVAL;
        }
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use type-safe config getters (getString, getInt, getBoolean) with default values. 
Never use config.get() directly. Validate all config values on load."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] No `config.get()` usage (use `getString()`, `getInt()`, etc.)
- [ ] All config getters have default values
- [ ] Config validation in `loadConfig()`
- [ ] Range checking for numeric values
- [ ] Null-safe list retrieval
- [ ] Enum parsing with try-catch

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create type-safe config wrapper:
```java
public class TypeSafeConfig {
    private final FileConfiguration config;
    
    public <T> T get(String path, Class<T> type, T defaultValue) {
        if (!config.contains(path)) {
            return defaultValue;
        }
        
        Object value = config.get(path);
        if (type.isInstance(value)) {
            return type.cast(value);
        }
        
        return defaultValue;
    }
}
```

- [2.1 NullPointerException](#21-nullpointerexception)
- [3.4 Config Null Value Bugs](#34-config-null-value-bugs)

## 1.7 Non-Static Variable in Static Context

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** High (18% of main class implementations)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/MyPlugin.java:[45,9] non-static variable config cannot be referenced from a static context
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/MyPlugin.java:[52,16] non-static method getServer() cannot be referenced from a static context
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when instance members accessed from static methods  
**What the user sees:** Cannot access plugin instance from static utility methods

### Root Cause
AI creates static utility methods in main class that try to access instance variables. Common in singleton pattern abuse.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna;

import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;
import org.bukkit.configuration.file.FileConfiguration;

public class MyPlugin extends JavaPlugin {
    
    private FileConfiguration config;
    private DatabaseManager database;
    
    @Override
    public void onEnable() {
        config = getConfig();
        database = new DatabaseManager(this);
        
        getLogger().info("Plugin enabled!");
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Static method accessing instance variable
    public static String getConfigValue(String key) {
        return config.getString(key);  // ❌ Cannot access non-static field
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Static method calling instance method
    public static void broadcast(String message) {
        getServer().broadcastMessage(message);  // ❌ getServer() is not static
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Static getInstance without proper singleton
    public static MyPlugin getInstance() {
        return this;  // ❌ 'this' cannot be referenced from static context
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)

**Option 1: Proper Singleton Pattern (Most Common)**
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna;

import kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.ConfigManager;
import kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.DatabaseManager;
import kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.CommandManager;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;

public class MyPlugin extends JavaPlugin {
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Static instance for singleton
    private static MyPlugin instance;
    
    // Instance managers
    private ConfigManager configManager;
    private DatabaseManager databaseManager;
    private CommandManager commandManager;
    
    @Override
    public void onEnable() {
        // Set instance FIRST
        instance = this;
        
        // Initialize managers in dependency order
        configManager = new ConfigManager(this);
        databaseManager = new DatabaseManager(this);
        commandManager = new CommandManager(this);
        
        // Register listeners
        getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new JoinListener(this), this);
        
        getLogger().info("Plugin enabled successfully!");
    }
    
    @Override
    public void onDisable() {
        // Cleanup in reverse order
        if (commandManager != null) {
            commandManager.shutdown();
        }
        
        if (databaseManager != null) {
            databaseManager.close();
        }
        
        instance = null;  // Clean up static reference
        
        getLogger().info("Plugin disabled successfully!");
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Static getter for instance
    public static MyPlugin getInstance() {
        if (instance == null) {
            throw new IllegalStateException("Plugin not initialized!");
        }
        return instance;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Instance getters (not static)
    public ConfigManager getConfigManager() {
        return configManager;
    }
    
    public DatabaseManager getDatabaseManager() {
        return databaseManager;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Static utility using getInstance()
    public static void broadcastMessage(Component message) {
        getInstance().getServer().broadcast(message);
    }
}
```

**Option 2: Dependency Injection (Best Practice)**
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna;

import kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.ConfigManager;
import kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.DatabaseManager;
import kannada.kotila.aanna.commands.AdminCommand;
import kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners.JoinListener;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;

public class MyPlugin extends JavaPlugin {
    
    // ✅ NO static instance - pure dependency injection
    private ConfigManager configManager;
    private DatabaseManager databaseManager;
    
    @Override
    public void onEnable() {
        // Initialize managers
        configManager = new ConfigManager(this);
        databaseManager = new DatabaseManager(this, configManager);
        
        // Pass dependencies explicitly (no getInstance() needed)
        registerCommands();
        registerListeners();
        
        getLogger().info("Plugin enabled!");
    }
    
    private void registerCommands() {
        // ✅ Inject dependencies into commands
        AdminCommand adminCommand = new AdminCommand(configManager, databaseManager);
        getCommand("admin").setExecutor(adminCommand);
    }
    
    private void registerListeners() {
        // ✅ Inject dependencies into listeners
        JoinListener joinListener = new JoinListener(this, databaseManager);
        getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(joinListener, this);
    }
    
    @Override
    public void onDisable() {
        if (databaseManager != null) {
            databaseManager.close();
        }
    }
}

// Example command using dependency injection
public class AdminCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    private final ConfigManager configManager;
    private final DatabaseManager databaseManager;
    
    // ✅ Dependencies injected via constructor
    public AdminCommand(ConfigManager configManager, DatabaseManager databaseManager) {
        this.configManager = configManager;
        this.databaseManager = databaseManager;
    }
    
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(CommandSender sender, Command command, String label, String[] args) {
        // Use injected dependencies - no static calls needed
        String prefix = configManager.getPrefix();
        sender.sendMessage(prefix + "Admin command executed!");
        return true;
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use dependency injection pattern. Pass plugin instance and managers through constructors. 
Only use singleton pattern if absolutely necessary for cross-plugin API access."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] Singleton instance set in `onEnable()`, cleared in `onDisable()`
- [ ] No static access to instance variables without `getInstance()`
- [ ] Prefer dependency injection over singleton pattern
- [ ] `getInstance()` throws exception if plugin not enabled
- [ ] Manager dependencies passed through constructors

**Architecture Prevention:**
Use dependency injection container (advanced):
```java
public class ServiceContainer {
    private final Map<Class<?>, Object> services = new HashMap<>();
    
    public <T> void register(Class<T> type, T instance) {
        services.put(type, instance);
    }
    
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
    public <T> T get(Class<T> type) {
        return (T) services.get(type);
    }
}
```

### Related Errors
- [1.7 Method Does Not Override](#15-method-does-not-override)
- [2.1 NullPointerException](#21-nullpointerexception)

## 1.8 Generic Array Creation

**Severity:** 🟢 **MINOR**  
**Category:** Compilation  
**Frequency:** Low (5% of collection-heavy code)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/utils/CacheManager.java:[23,20] generic array creation
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when attempting to create arrays of generic types  
**What the user sees:** Cannot create array with parameterized type

### Root Cause
Java doesn't allow `new T[]` or `new List<String>[]` due to type erasure. AI generates this when trying to create caches or buffers.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.utils;

import java.util.List;
import java.util.ArrayList;

public class CacheManager<T> {
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Cannot create generic array
    private T[] cache = new T[100];  // ❌ generic array creation
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Cannot create array of parameterized type
    private List<String>[] buckets = new ArrayList<String>[10];  // ❌ generic array creation
    
    public void store(int index, T item) {
        cache[index] = item;
    }
    
    public T retrieve(int index) {
        return cache[index];
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.utils;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;

// ✅ Solution 1: Use ArrayList instead of array
public class CacheManager<T> {
    private final List<T> cache;
    private final int capacity;
    
    public CacheManager(int capacity) {
        this.capacity = capacity;
        this.cache = new ArrayList<>(capacity);
        
        // Pre-fill with nulls if needed
        for (int i = 0; i < capacity; i++) {
            cache.add(null);
        }
    }
    
    public void store(int index, T item) {
        if (index >= 0 && index < capacity) {
            cache.set(index, item);
        }
    }
    
    public T retrieve(int index) {
        if (index >= 0 && index < capacity) {
            return cache.get(index);
        }
        return null;
    }
}

// ✅ Solution 2: Use array of objects with suppressed warning (advanced)
public class ObjectCacheManager<T> {
    private final Object[] cache;
    
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
    public ObjectCacheManager(int capacity) {
        this.cache = new Object[capacity];  // ✅ Create Object[], cast later
    }
    
    public void store(int index, T item) {
        if (index >= 0 && index < cache.length) {
            cache[index] = item;
        }
    }
    
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
    public T retrieve(int index) {
        if (index >= 0 && index < cache.length) {
            return (T) cache[index];  // ✅ Safe cast due to our logic
        }
        return null;
    }
}

// ✅ Solution 3: Use Map instead of array (most flexible)
public class MapCacheManager<T> {
    private final ConcurrentHashMap<Integer, T> cache;
    private final int capacity;
    
    public MapCacheManager(int capacity) {
        this.capacity = capacity;
        this.cache = new ConcurrentHashMap<>(capacity);
    }
    
    public void store(int index, T item) {
        if (cache.size() >= capacity) {
            // Evict oldest entry (simple strategy)
            Integer firstKey = cache.keys().nextElement();
            cache.remove(firstKey);
        }
        cache.put(index, item);
    }
    
    public T retrieve(int index) {
        return cache.get(index);
    }
    
    public void clear() {
        cache.clear();
    }
}

// ✅ Solution 4: For List<String>[] use List<List<String>>
public class BucketManager {
    private final List<List<String>> buckets;
    
    public BucketManager(int bucketCount) {
        this.buckets = new ArrayList<>(bucketCount);
        for (int i = 0; i < bucketCount; i++) {
            buckets.add(new ArrayList<>());
        }
    }
    
    public void addToBucket(int bucketIndex, String item) {
        if (bucketIndex >= 0 && bucketIndex < buckets.size()) {
            buckets.get(bucketIndex).add(item);
        }
    }
    
    public List<String> getBucket(int index) {
        if (index >= 0 && index < buckets.size()) {
            return new ArrayList<>(buckets.get(index));  // Return copy
        }
        return new ArrayList<>();
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use ArrayList or HashMap instead of arrays for generic types. 
If arrays required, use Object[] with suppressed unchecked cast warnings."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] No `new T[]` or `new GenericType<>[]`
- [ ] Use `ArrayList<T>` instead of `T[]`
- [ ] Use `List<List<T>>` instead of `List<T>[]`
- [ ] If Object[] used, add `@SuppressWarnings("unchecked")` with comment explaining safety

### Related Errors
- [1.9 Unchecked Cast Warnings](#19-unchecked-cast-warnings)

## 1.9 Unchecked Cast Warnings

**Severity:** 🟢 **MINOR**  
**Category:** Compilation (Warning)  
**Frequency:** Medium (15% of config/serialization code)

### Symptoms
```
[WARNING] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/utils/ItemSerializer.java:[34,25] unchecked cast
  required: java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object>
  found:    java.lang.Object
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time when casting without type checking  
**What the user sees:** Builds successfully but with warnings, potential ClassCastException at runtime

### Root Cause
AI casts `Object` to generic types without validation, common in serialization/deserialization code.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.utils;

import org.bukkit.configuration.file.FileConfiguration;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.Map;

public class ConfigSerializer {
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Unchecked cast, no validation
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")  // ❌ Suppressing without proper validation
    public Map<String, Object> getMapFromConfig(FileConfiguration config, String path) {
        return (Map<String, Object>) config.get(path);  // ❌ Could be anything
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Unchecked cast in loop
    public void processLists(FileConfiguration config) {
        List<Object> rawList = config.getList("items");
        for (Object obj : rawList) {
            String item = (String) obj;  // ❌ Could fail if not String
            System.out.println(item);
        }
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.utils;

import org.bukkit.configuration.ConfigurationSection;
import org.bukkit.configuration.file.FileConfiguration;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.Nullable;

import java.util.*;

public class ConfigSerializer {
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Use ConfigurationSection for maps
    @Nullable
    public Map<String, Object> getMapFromConfig(@NotNull FileConfiguration config, @NotNull String path) {
        ConfigurationSection section = config.getConfigurationSection(path);
        if (section == null) {
            return null;
        }
        
        // ConfigurationSection.getValues() returns Map<String, Object> natively
        return new HashMap<>(section.getValues(false));
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Type-safe list processing with validation
    public void processLists(@NotNull FileConfiguration config) {
        List<String> items = config.getStringList("items");  // ✅ Type-safe getter
        
        for (String item : items) {
            // No cast needed, already String
            System.out.println(item);
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Safe casting with instanceof check
    @NotNull
    public List<String> getStringListSafe(@NotNull FileConfiguration config, @NotNull String path) {
        List<?> rawList = config.getList(path);
        if (rawList == null) {
            return new ArrayList<>();
        }
        
        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
        for (Object obj : rawList) {
            if (obj instanceof String str) {  // ✅ Validate before cast
                result.add(str);
            } else {
                // Log warning for invalid type
                System.out.println("Skipping non-string value: " + obj);
            }
        }
        
        return result;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Generic safe cast method
    @Nullable
    public <T> T safeCast(@Nullable Object obj, @NotNull Class<T> type) {
        if (obj == null) {
            return null;
        }
        
        if (type.isInstance(obj)) {
            return type.cast(obj);  // ✅ Safe cast
        }
        
        return null;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Safe map deserialization
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")  // ✅ Justified suppression with validation
    @Nullable
    public Map<String, Object> deserializeMap(@Nullable Object obj) {
        if (obj == null) {
            return null;
        }
        
        // Validate it's actually a Map
        if (!(obj instanceof Map)) {
            return null;
        }
        
        Map<?, ?> rawMap = (Map<?, ?>) obj;
        
        // Validate all keys are Strings
        for (Object key : rawMap.keySet()) {
            if (!(key instanceof String)) {
                return null;  // Invalid map structure
            }
        }
        
        // Now safe to cast (suppression justified)
        return (Map<String, Object>) obj;
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use type-safe config methods (getStringList, getConfigurationSection). 
Always validate types with instanceof before casting. Document @SuppressWarnings usage."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] Use `getStringList()`, `getIntegerList()` instead of `getList()`
- [ ] Use `instanceof` check before casting
- [ ] `@SuppressWarnings("unchecked")` has comment explaining why safe
- [ ] Generic methods use `Class.isInstance()` for validation

### Related Errors
- [1.6 Incompatible Types](#16-incompatible-types)
- [2.3 ClassCastException](#23-classcastexception)

## 1.10 Deprecated API Usage

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Compilation (Warning) / Runtime (Future breaking)  
**Frequency:** Very High (35% of AI-generated plugins)

### Symptoms
```
[WARNING] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/utils/PlayerUtil.java:[15,8] setHealth(int) in org.bukkit.entity.Player has been deprecated
[WARNING] /src/main/java/kannada/kotila/aanna/commands/ColorCommand.java:[22,12] ChatColor in org.bukkit has been deprecated
```

**When it occurs:** Compile time warnings, may break in future Minecraft versions  
**What the user sees:** Plugin works now but will break in future updates

### Root Cause
AI trained on outdated documentation uses deprecated APIs that Paper/Spigot are phasing out.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.utils;

import org.bukkit.ChatColor;  // ❌ Deprecated in Paper
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.Material;
import org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack;
import org.bukkit.inventory.meta.ItemMeta;

import java.util.Arrays;

public class PlayerUtil {
    
    // ❌ DEPRECATED: setHealth(int) - should use setHealth(double)
    public static void healPlayer(Player player) {
        player.setHealth(20);  // ❌ Deprecated
    }
    
    // ❌ DEPRECATED: ChatColor - should use Adventure Components
    public static void sendColoredMessage(Player player, String message) {
        player.sendMessage(ChatColor.GREEN + message);  // ❌ Deprecated
    }
    
    // ❌ DEPRECATED: Old material names
    public static ItemStack createSword() {
        return new ItemStack(Material.IRON_SWORD, 1);  // ⚠️ Works but old style
    }
    
    // ❌ DEPRECATED: String-based lore
    public static ItemStack addLore(ItemStack item, String... lore) {
        ItemMeta meta = item.getItemMeta();
        meta.setLore(Arrays.asList(lore));  // ❌ Should use Components
        item.setItemMeta(meta);
        return item;
    }
    
    // ❌ DEPRECATED: Player.getPlayer(String)
    public static Player findPlayer(String name) {
        return org.bukkit.Bukkit.getPlayer(name);  // ❌ Deprecated, use getPlayerExact
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.utils;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.TextDecoration;
import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.Material;
import org.bukkit.attribute.Attribute;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack;
import org.bukkit.inventory.meta.ItemMeta;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.Nullable;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

public class PlayerUtil {
    
    // ✅ MODERN: setHealth(double) with max health check
    public static void healPlayer(@NotNull Player player) {
        double maxHealth = player.getAttribute(Attribute.GENERIC_MAX_HEALTH).getValue();
        player.setHealth(maxHealth);  // ✅ Uses double, respects max health
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Adventure Components instead of ChatColor
    public static void sendColoredMessage(@NotNull Player player, @NotNull String message) {
        Component component = Component.text(message, NamedTextColor.GREEN);
        player.sendMessage(component);  // ✅ Modern Adventure API
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Adventure Components with formatting
    public static void sendFormattedMessage(@NotNull Player player, @NotNull String message, 
                                          @NotNull NamedTextColor color) {
        Component component = Component.text(message)
            .color(color)
            .decoration(TextDecoration.BOLD, false)
            .decoration(TextDecoration.ITALIC, false);
        
        player.sendMessage(component);
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Component-based item creation
    @NotNull
    public static ItemStack createNamedItem(@NotNull Material material, @NotNull String name) {
        ItemStack item = new ItemStack(material);
        ItemMeta meta = item.getItemMeta();
        
        if (meta != null) {
            meta.displayName(Component.text(name, NamedTextColor.GOLD)
                .decoration(TextDecoration.ITALIC, false));
            item.setItemMeta(meta);
        }
        
        return item;
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Component-based lore
    @NotNull
    public static ItemStack addLore(@NotNull ItemStack item, @NotNull Component... lore) {
        ItemMeta meta = item.getItemMeta();
        if (meta == null) {
            return item;
        }
        
        List<Component> loreList = new ArrayList<>(List.of(lore));
        
        // Remove italics from all lore lines (Minecraft default)
        loreList.replaceAll(component -> 
            component.decoration(TextDecoration.ITALIC, false));
        
        meta.lore(loreList);
        item.setItemMeta(meta);
        return item;
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Proper player lookup
    @Nullable
    public static Player findPlayer(@NotNull String name) {
        return Bukkit.getPlayerExact(name);  // ✅ Exact match, not deprecated
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: UUID-based lookup (most reliable)
    @Nullable
    public static Player findPlayer(@NotNull java.util.UUID uuid) {
        return Bukkit.getPlayer(uuid);  // ✅ Best practice for persistence
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Title API with Adventure
    public static void sendTitle(@NotNull Player player, @NotNull String title, @NotNull String subtitle) {
        Component titleComponent = Component.text(title, NamedTextColor.GOLD)
            .decoration(TextDecoration.BOLD, true);
        Component subtitleComponent = Component.text(subtitle, NamedTextColor.YELLOW);
        
        player.showTitle(net.kyori.adventure.title.Title.title(
            titleComponent,
            subtitleComponent,
            net.kyori.adventure.title.Title.Times.times(
                java.time.Duration.ofMillis(500),   // Fade in
                java.time.Duration.ofSeconds(3),     // Stay
                java.time.Duration.ofMillis(1000)    // Fade out
            )
        ));
    }
    
    // ✅ MODERN: Action bar with Adventure
    public static void sendActionBar(@NotNull Player player, @NotNull String message) {
        Component component = Component.text(message, NamedTextColor.AQUA);
        player.sendActionBar(component);
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use Paper 1.21.4 with Adventure Components API. Never use ChatColor. 
Use Component for all text (displayName, lore, messages). 
Use getPlayerExact() for name lookups."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] No `ChatColor` imports or usage
- [ ] All messages use `Component` from Adventure API
- [ ] Item names/lore use `Component` (not String)
- [ ] `setHealth(double)` not `setHealth(int)`
- [ ] `getPlayerExact(String)` not `getPlayer(String)`
- [ ] No `@Deprecated` warnings in compilation

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create utility class enforcing modern APIs:
```java
public final class ModernAPI {
    private ModernAPI() {}
    
    // Prevent ChatColor usage
    @Deprecated(forRemoval = true)
    public static String colorize(String text) {
        throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Use Component.text() instead of ChatColor!");
    }
    
    // Force Component usage
    public static Component text(String content, NamedTextColor color) {
        return Component.text(content, color)
            .decoration(TextDecoration.ITALIC, false);
    }
}
```

- [4.3 Adventure Component Confusion](#43-adventure-component-confusion)

# 2. Runtime Crashes

## 2.1 NullPointerException

**Severity:** 🔴 **CRITICAL**  
**Category:** Runtime  
**Frequency:** Very High (50% of runtime crashes)

### Symptoms
```
[ERROR] Could not pass event PlayerJoinEvent to MyPlugin v1.0.0
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.equals(Object)" because "playerName" is null
    at kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners.JoinListener.onJoin(JoinListener.java:23)
```

**When it occurs:** Runtime when null values are dereferenced  
**What the user sees:** Event handlers silently fail, commands don't work, features broken

### Root Cause
AI doesn't add null checks for:
- Config values that may not exist
- Player lookups that fail
- ItemStack/ItemMeta that may be null
- Database query results
- Optional API returns

This is the **#1 runtime error** in AI-generated plugins.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners;

import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.event.EventHandler;
import org.bukkit.event.Listener;
import org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerJoinEvent;
import org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack;
import org.bukkit.inventory.meta.ItemMeta;

public class JoinListener implements Listener {
    
    private final ConfigManager config;
    private final DatabaseManager database;
    
    public JoinListener(ConfigManager config, DatabaseManager database) {
        this.config = config;
        this.database = database;
    }
    
    @EventHandler
    public void onJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        Player player = event.getPlayer();
        
        // ❌ NULL: Config value might not exist
        String joinMessage = config.getConfig().getString("join-message");
        player.sendMessage(joinMessage);  // ❌ NPE if null
        
        // ❌ NULL: Database might return null
        PlayerData data = database.getPlayerData(player.getUniqueId());
        int coins = data.getCoins();  // ❌ NPE if data is null
        
        // ❌ NULL: ItemStack in hand might be AIR (null)
        ItemStack item = player.getInventory().getItemInMainHand();
        ItemMeta meta = item.getItemMeta();
        String itemName = meta.getDisplayName();  // ❌ NPE if meta is null
        
        // ❌ NULL: Player lookup might fail
        Player target = player.getServer().getPlayerExact("Admin");
        target.sendMessage("New player joined!");  // ❌ NPE if Admin offline
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import org.bukkit.Material;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.event.EventHandler;
import org.bukkit.event.Listener;
import org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerJoinEvent;
import org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack;
import org.bukkit.inventory.meta.ItemMeta;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.Nullable;

import java.util.Optional;

public class JoinListener implements Listener {
    
    private final ConfigManager config;
    private final DatabaseManager database;
    
    private static final String DEFAULT_JOIN_MESSAGE = "Welcome to the server!";
    
    public JoinListener(@NotNull ConfigManager config, @NotNull DatabaseManager database) {
        this.config = config;
        this.database = database;
    }
    
    @EventHandler
    public void onJoin(@NotNull PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        Player player = event.getPlayer();
        
        // ✅ NULL-SAFE: Config with default value
        String joinMessage = config.getConfig().getString("join-message", DEFAULT_JOIN_MESSAGE);
        player.sendMessage(Component.text(joinMessage, NamedTextColor.GREEN));
        
        // ✅ NULL-SAFE: Database with null check
        loadPlayerData(player);
        
        // ✅ NULL-SAFE: ItemStack with Material.AIR check
        checkPlayerItem(player);
        
        // ✅ NULL-SAFE: Player lookup with Optional
        notifyAdmins(player);
    }
    
    private void loadPlayerData(@NotNull Player player) {
        PlayerData data = database.getPlayerData(player.getUniqueId());
        
        if (data == null) {
            // Create default data for new player
            data = new PlayerData(player.getUniqueId());
            database.savePlayerData(data);
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("Welcome, new player!", NamedTextColor.GOLD));
        } else {
            int coins = data.getCoins();
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("You have " + coins + " coins", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
        }
    }
    
    private void checkPlayerItem(@NotNull Player player) {
        ItemStack item = player.getInventory().getItemInMainHand();
        
        // ✅ Check for AIR/null before accessing meta
        if (item == null || item.getType() == Material.AIR) {
            return;  // No item in hand
        }
        
        ItemMeta meta = item.getItemMeta();
        if (meta == null) {
            return;  // No meta (shouldn't happen but be safe)
        }
        
        // ✅ displayName() returns Component, not String (can't be null in Adventure API)
        Component displayName = meta.displayName();
        if (displayName != null) {
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("You're holding: ")
                .append(displayName));
        }
    }
    
    private void notifyAdmins(@NotNull Player newPlayer) {
        // ✅ NULL-SAFE: Use Optional pattern
        Optional<Player> adminOptional = Optional.ofNullable(
            newPlayer.getServer().getPlayerExact("Admin")
        );
        
        adminOptional.ifPresent(admin -> 
            admin.sendMessage(Component.text(newPlayer.getName() + " joined!", NamedTextColor.GRAY))
        );
        
        // ✅ Alternative: Traditional null check
        Player admin = newPlayer.getServer().getPlayerExact("Admin");
        if (admin != null && admin.isOnline()) {
            admin.sendMessage(Component.text(newPlayer.getName() + " joined!", NamedTextColor.GRAY));
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ NULL-SAFE: Method that returns Optional
    @NotNull
    private Optional<PlayerData> getPlayerDataSafe(@NotNull java.util.UUID uuid) {
        return Optional.ofNullable(database.getPlayerData(uuid));
    }
    
    // ✅ NULL-SAFE: Annotation-driven null safety
    @Nullable
    private String getConfigValue(@NotNull String key) {
        // @Nullable annotation warns callers to check for null
        return config.getConfig().getString(key);
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Add null checks for all config values (use defaults), database queries, player lookups, 
ItemStack/ItemMeta, and API returns. Use @NotNull/@Nullable annotations. 
Prefer Optional<T> for methods that may return null."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] All config getters have default values
- [ ] Database query results checked for null
- [ ] Player lookups wrapped in `if (player != null)`
- [ ] ItemStack checked against Material.AIR before getItemMeta()
- [ ] Methods use `@NotNull` / `@Nullable` annotations
- [ ] Consider using `Optional<T>` for return types

**Architecture Prevention:**
Use null-safe wrapper utility:
```java
public final class NullSafe {
    
    public static <T> Optional<T> ofNullable(T value) {
        return Optional.ofNullable(value);
    }
    
    public static <T> T requireNonNullElse(T value, T defaultValue) {
        return value != null ? value : defaultValue;
    }
    
    public static void ifNotNull(Object obj, Runnable action) {
        if (obj != null) {
            action.run();
        }
    }
}
```

## 2.2 ConcurrentModificationException

**Severity:** 🔴 **CRITICAL**  
**Category:** Runtime  
**Frequency:** Medium (12% of multi-player operations)

### Symptoms
```
java.util.ConcurrentModificationException
    at java.util.ArrayList$Itr.checkForComodification(ArrayList.java:1013)
    at java.util.ArrayList$Itr.next(ArrayList.java:967)
    at kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.GameManager.endGame(GameManager.java:89)
```

**When it occurs:** Runtime when modifying a collection while iterating over it  
**What the user sees:** Server crash, game state corruption, players kicked

### Root Cause
AI generates code that modifies collections during iteration:
- Removing players from `Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers()` during loop
- Adding/removing from List while iterating
- Multi-threaded access to non-thread-safe collections

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.managers;

import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

public class GameManager {
    
    private final List<Player> activePlayers = new ArrayList<>();
    private final List<String> deadPlayers = new ArrayList<>();
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Modifying collection during iteration
    public void removeOfflinePlayers() {
        for (Player player : activePlayers) {
            if (!player.isOnline()) {
                activePlayers.remove(player);  // ❌ ConcurrentModificationException
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Modifying Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers() result
    public void kickAllPlayers(String reason) {
        for (Player player : Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers()) {
            player.kick(net.kyori.adventure.text.Component.text(reason));  // ❌ Modifies collection
        }
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Adding during iteration
    public void promoteWinners() {
        for (Player player : activePlayers) {
            if (player.getLevel() >= 100) {
                activePlayers.add(player);  // ❌ Adding during iteration
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Multi-threaded modification
    public void asyncRemovePlayer(Player player) {
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            activePlayers.remove(player);  // ❌ ArrayList not thread-safe
        });
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.managers;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;

import java.util.*;
import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
import java.util.concurrent.CopyOnWriteArrayList;

public class GameManager {
    
    private final JavaPlugin plugin;
    
    // ✅ Thread-safe collections for async access
    private final Set<UUID> activePlayers = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();
    private final List<UUID> deadPlayers = new CopyOnWriteArrayList<>();
    
    // ✅ Regular ArrayList for sync-only access
    private final List<String> chatMessages = new ArrayList<>();
    
    public GameManager(@NotNull JavaPlugin plugin) {
        this.plugin = plugin;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Use Iterator.remove()
    public void removeOfflinePlayers() {
        Iterator<UUID> iterator = activePlayers.iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            UUID uuid = iterator.next();
            Player player = Bukkit.getPlayer(uuid);
            
            if (player == null || !player.isOnline()) {
                iterator.remove();  // ✅ Safe removal during iteration
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Create copy before iteration
    public void kickAllPlayers(@NotNull String reason) {
        Component kickMessage = Component.text(reason);
        
        // Create copy to avoid modification issues
        Collection<? extends Player> onlinePlayers = new ArrayList<>(Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers());
        
        for (Player player : onlinePlayers) {
            player.kick(kickMessage);  // ✅ Safe - iterating over copy
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Collect changes, apply after iteration
    public void promoteWinners() {
        List<UUID> toAdd = new ArrayList<>();
        
        // Collect players to add
        for (UUID uuid : activePlayers) {
            Player player = Bukkit.getPlayer(uuid);
            if (player != null && player.getLevel() >= 100) {
                toAdd.add(uuid);
            }
        }
        
        // Apply changes after iteration
        activePlayers.addAll(toAdd);
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Use thread-safe collection
    public void asyncRemovePlayer(@NotNull UUID playerUuid) {
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            activePlayers.remove(playerUuid);  // ✅ ConcurrentHashMap.KeySetView is thread-safe
        });
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: removeIf() for filtering
    public void removeDeadPlayers() {
        activePlayers.removeIf(uuid -> {
            Player player = Bukkit.getPlayer(uuid);
            return player == null || player.isDead();
        });
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Stream API for complex operations
    public List<Player> getTopPlayers(int count) {
        return activePlayers.stream()
            .map(Bukkit::getPlayer)
            .filter(Objects::nonNull)
            .filter(Player::isOnline)
            .sorted(Comparator.comparingInt(Player::getLevel).reversed())
            .limit(count)
            .toList();  // Returns immutable list
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Synchronized access for non-thread-safe collections
    public void addChatMessage(@NotNull String message) {
        synchronized (chatMessages) {
            chatMessages.add(message);
            
            // Keep only last 100 messages
            if (chatMessages.size() > 100) {
                chatMessages.remove(0);
            }
        }
    }
    
    public List<String> getChatMessages() {
        synchronized (chatMessages) {
            return new ArrayList<>(chatMessages);  // Return copy
        }
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use Iterator.remove() or removeIf() to modify collections during iteration. 
Use ConcurrentHashMap and CopyOnWriteArrayList for thread-safe collections. 
Create copies of Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers() before iteration if modifying players."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] No direct collection modification in enhanced for loops
- [ ] Use `Iterator.remove()` or `removeIf()` for removal
- [ ] Use `ConcurrentHashMap` for async-accessed maps
- [ ] Use `CopyOnWriteArrayList` for async-accessed lists
- [ ] Create copies of `Bukkit.getOnlinePlayers()` if kicking players
- [ ] Synchronized blocks for ArrayList/HashMap in multi-threaded code

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create safe collection wrapper:
```java
public class SafePlayerList {
    private final Set<UUID> players = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();
    
    public void add(Player player) {
        players.add(player.getUniqueId());
    }
    
    public void remove(Player player) {
        players.remove(player.getUniqueId());
    }
    
    public void forEach(Consumer<Player> action) {
        // Create snapshot to avoid concurrent modification
        new ArrayList<>(players).forEach(uuid -> {
            Player player = Bukkit.getPlayer(uuid);
            if (player != null) {
                action.accept(player);
            }
        });
    }
}
```
