## 2.3 ClassCastException

**Severity:** 🟡 **MAJOR**  
**Category:** Runtime  
**Frequency:** Medium (10% of entity/inventory code)

### Symptoms
```
java.lang.ClassCastException: class org.bukkit.craftbukkit.v1_21_R1.entity.CraftZombie cannot be cast to class org.bukkit.entity.Player
    at kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners.DamageListener.onDamage(DamageListener.java:34)
```

**When it occurs:** Runtime when casting to wrong type without instanceof check  
**What the user sees:** Event handlers crash when unexpected entity types trigger events

### Root Cause
AI casts entities, inventory holders, or commands without validating type first.

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners;

import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.entity.Entity;
import org.bukkit.event.EventHandler;
import org.bukkit.event.Listener;
import org.bukkit.event.entity.EntityDamageByEntityEvent;
import org.bukkit.event.inventory.InventoryClickEvent;
import org.bukkit.inventory.InventoryHolder;

public class DamageListener implements Listener {
    
    // ❌ WRONG: No instanceof check before cast
    @EventHandler
    public void onDamage(EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        Entity damager = event.getDamager();
        
        // ❌ Could be arrow, snowball, zombie, etc.
        Player player = (Player) damager;  // ❌ ClassCastException
        player.sendMessage("You hit something!");
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Assuming entity is always Player
    @EventHandler
    public void onEntityDamage(EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        Player victim = (Player) event.getEntity();  // ❌ Could be any entity
        victim.sendMessage("You were hit!");
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Inventory holder might not be Player
    @EventHandler
    public void onClick(InventoryClickEvent event) {
        InventoryHolder holder = event.getInventory().getHolder();
        Player player = (Player) holder;  // ❌ Could be Chest, Hopper, etc.
        player.sendMessage("Clicked!");
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.listeners;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import org.bukkit.entity.*;
import org.bukkit.event.EventHandler;
import org.bukkit.event.Listener;
import org.bukkit.event.entity.EntityDamageByEntityEvent;
import org.bukkit.event.inventory.InventoryClickEvent;
import org.bukkit.inventory.InventoryHolder;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;

public class DamageListener implements Listener {
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Pattern matching instanceof (Java 16+)
    @EventHandler
    public void onDamage(@NotNull EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        Entity damager = event.getDamager();
        
        // ✅ Type check with pattern matching
        if (damager instanceof Player player) {
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("You hit something!", NamedTextColor.GREEN));
            
            // Check what was hit
            if (event.getEntity() instanceof Player victim) {
                victim.sendMessage(Component.text("You were hit by " + player.getName(), NamedTextColor.RED));
            }
        } else if (damager instanceof Arrow arrow) {
            // Handle arrow damage
            if (arrow.getShooter() instanceof Player shooter) {
                shooter.sendMessage(Component.text("Your arrow hit!", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
            }
        } else if (damager instanceof Snowball snowball) {
            // Handle snowball
            if (snowball.getShooter() instanceof Player thrower) {
                thrower.sendMessage(Component.text("Direct hit!", NamedTextColor.AQUA));
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Multiple type checks
    @EventHandler
    public void onEntityDamage(@NotNull EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        Entity entity = event.getEntity();
        
        // ✅ Comprehensive type checking
        switch (entity) {
            case Player player -> handlePlayerDamage(player, event);
            case Villager villager -> handleVillagerDamage(villager, event);
            case Monster monster -> handleMonsterDamage(monster, event);
            default -> {
                // Handle other entities or ignore
            }
        }
    }
    
    private void handlePlayerDamage(@NotNull Player player, @NotNull EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        double damage = event.getFinalDamage();
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("You took " + damage + " damage!", NamedTextColor.RED));
    }
    
    private void handleVillagerDamage(@NotNull Villager villager, @NotNull EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        // Protect villagers
        if (event.getDamager() instanceof Player attacker) {
            attacker.sendMessage(Component.text("Don't hurt villagers!", NamedTextColor.RED));
            event.setCancelled(true);
        }
    }
    
    private void handleMonsterDamage(@NotNull Monster monster, @NotNull EntityDamageByEntityEvent event) {
        // Double damage to monsters
        event.setDamage(event.getDamage() * 2);
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Inventory holder type checking
    @EventHandler
    public void onClick(@NotNull InventoryClickEvent event) {
        InventoryHolder holder = event.getInventory().getHolder();
        
        // ✅ Check if holder is a Player
        if (holder instanceof Player player) {
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("You clicked your inventory", NamedTextColor.GRAY));
        } else if (holder instanceof Chest) {
            // It's a chest inventory
            if (event.getWhoClicked() instanceof Player player) {
                player.sendMessage(Component.text("Clicked chest", NamedTextColor.GRAY));
            }
        } else {
            // Other inventory types (Hopper, Furnace, etc.)
        }
        
        // ✅ The clicker is always HumanEntity, but check for Player
        if (event.getWhoClicked() instanceof Player player) {
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("Inventory action", NamedTextColor.GRAY));
        }
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Safe entity cast with fallback
    private void processEntity(@NotNull Entity entity) {
        String message = switch (entity) {
            case Player player -> "Player: " + player.getName();
            case Zombie zombie -> "Zombie";
            case Creeper creeper -> "Creeper";
            case Arrow arrow -> "Arrow";
            default -> "Unknown entity: " + entity.getType();
        };
        
        // Log or process
        System.out.println(message);
    }
}
```

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Always use instanceof pattern matching before casting entities. 
Use switch expressions for multiple type checks. 
Never cast Entity to Player without validation."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] All entity casts preceded by `instanceof` check
- [ ] Use Java 16+ pattern matching: `if (entity instanceof Player player)`
- [ ] Check `Arrow.getShooter()` instanceof Player before cast
- [ ] Validate InventoryHolder type before casting
- [ ] Use switch expressions for multiple type checks

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create type-safe entity wrapper:
```java
public final class EntityUtil {
    
    public static Optional<Player> asPlayer(Entity entity) {
        return entity instanceof Player p ? Optional.of(p) : Optional.empty();
    }
    
    public static void ifPlayer(Entity entity, Consumer<Player> action) {
        if (entity instanceof Player player) {
            action.accept(player);
        }
    }
    
    public static <T extends Entity> Optional<T> asType(Entity entity, Class<T> type) {
        return type.isInstance(entity) ? Optional.of(type.cast(entity)) : Optional.empty();
    }
}
```

### Related Errors
- [1.9 Unchecked Cast Warnings](#19-unchecked-cast-warnings)
- [2.1 NullPointerException](#21-nullpointerexception)

---

## 2.4 IllegalStateException (Async)

**Severity:** 🔴 **CRITICAL**  
**Category:** Runtime  
**Frequency:** High (20% of database-heavy plugins)

### Symptoms
```
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Asynchronous entity world add!
    at org.bukkit.craftbukkit.v1_21_R1.CraftWorld.addEntity(CraftWorld.java:1234)
    at kannada.kotila.aanna.managers.SpawnManager.spawnReward(SpawnManager.java:45)
```

**When it occurs:** Runtime when calling sync-only Bukkit API from async thread  
**What the user sees:** Server crash, entities not spawning, world not modifying

### Root Cause
AI calls Bukkit API that requires main thread from async tasks:
- Spawning entities
- Modifying blocks
- Teleporting players
- Inventory operations

**Rule:** Almost all Bukkit API requires main thread except Player.sendMessage().

### Bad Code (What AI Generates)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.managers;

import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.Location;
import org.bukkit.Material;
import org.bukkit.entity.EntityType;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;

public class RewardManager {
    
    private final JavaPlugin plugin;
    private final DatabaseManager database;
    
    public RewardManager(JavaPlugin plugin, DatabaseManager database) {
        this.plugin = plugin;
        this.database = database;
    }
    
    // ❌ WRONG: Bukkit API called from async thread
    public void giveReward(Player player) {
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            // Async database query (correct)
            int coins = database.getCoins(player.getUniqueId());
            
            // ❌ WRONG: Spawning entity on async thread
            Location loc = player.getLocation();
            loc.getWorld().spawnEntity(loc, EntityType.EXPERIENCE_ORB);
            
            // ❌ WRONG: Modifying inventory on async thread
            player.getInventory().addItem(new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, coins));
            
            // ❌ WRONG: Teleporting on async thread
            player.teleport(loc.add(0, 10, 0));
            
            // ❌ WRONG: Block modification on async thread
            player.getLocation().getBlock().setType(Material.GOLD_BLOCK);
        });
    }
}
```

### Good Code (The Fix)
```java
package kannada.kotila.aanna.managers;

import net.kyori.adventure.text.Component;
import net.kyori.adventure.text.format.NamedTextColor;
import org.bukkit.Bukkit;
import org.bukkit.Location;
import org.bukkit.Material;
import org.bukkit.entity.EntityType;
import org.bukkit.entity.Player;
import org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack;
import org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin;
import org.jetbrains.annotations.NotNull;

import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;

public class RewardManager {
    
    private final JavaPlugin plugin;
    private final DatabaseManager database;
    
    public RewardManager(@NotNull JavaPlugin plugin, @NotNull DatabaseManager database) {
        this.plugin = plugin;
        this.database = database;
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Async → Sync pattern
    public void giveReward(@NotNull Player player) {
        // Step 1: Async database query
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            int coins = database.getCoins(player.getUniqueId());
            
            // Step 2: Switch back to main thread for Bukkit API
            Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask(plugin, () -> {
                // ✅ Now on main thread - safe to use Bukkit API
                Location loc = player.getLocation();
                
                // Spawn experience orb
                loc.getWorld().spawnEntity(loc, EntityType.EXPERIENCE_ORB);
                
                // Add items to inventory
                player.getInventory().addItem(new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, coins));
                
                // Send message (works on any thread)
                player.sendMessage(Component.text("You received " + coins + " diamonds!", NamedTextColor.GOLD));
            });
        });
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: CompletableFuture pattern (modern)
    public CompletableFuture<Void> giveRewardAsync(@NotNull Player player) {
        return CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(() -> {
            // Async: Database query
            return database.getCoins(player.getUniqueId());
        }).thenAcceptAsync(coins -> {
            // Sync: Bukkit API on main thread
            Location loc = player.getLocation();
            loc.getWorld().spawnEntity(loc, EntityType.EXPERIENCE_ORB);
            player.getInventory().addItem(new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, coins));
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("Reward given!", NamedTextColor.GREEN));
        }, runnable -> Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask(plugin, runnable));
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Paper's async scheduler (Paper 1.20.4+)
    public void giveRewardPaper(@NotNull Player player) {
        // Paper-specific async method
        plugin.getServer().getAsyncScheduler().runNow(plugin, task -> {
            int coins = database.getCoins(player.getUniqueId());
            
            // Switch to entity scheduler (thread-safe)
            player.getScheduler().run(plugin, scheduledTask -> {
                Location loc = player.getLocation();
                loc.getWorld().spawnEntity(loc, EntityType.EXPERIENCE_ORB);
                player.getInventory().addItem(new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, coins));
            }, null);
        });
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: Helper method for async→sync pattern
    private void runAsync(@NotNull Runnable asyncTask, @NotNull Runnable syncCallback) {
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            asyncTask.run();
            Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask(plugin, syncCallback);
        });
    }
    
    // Usage example
    public void exampleUsage(@NotNull Player player) {
        runAsync(
            // Async part
            () -> {
                int data = database.getCoins(player.getUniqueId());
                // Store data in closure or field
            },
            // Sync part
            () -> {
                player.sendMessage(Component.text("Data loaded!", NamedTextColor.GREEN));
            }
        );
    }
    
    // ✅ CORRECT: What CAN be done async
    public void safeAsyncOperations(@NotNull Player player) {
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            // ✅ SAFE: Sending messages
            player.sendMessage(Component.text("Processing...", NamedTextColor.YELLOW));
            
            // ✅ SAFE: Database queries
            database.getCoins(player.getUniqueId());
            
            // ✅ SAFE: File I/O
            // saveToFile();
            
            // ✅ SAFE: HTTP requests
            // fetchFromAPI();
            
            // ✅ SAFE: Heavy calculations
            // complexMathOperation();
        });
    }
}
```

### Thread Safety Reference Table
| Operation | Async Safe? | Notes |
|-----------|-------------|-------|
| `player.sendMessage()` | ✅ Yes | Thread-safe |
| `player.teleport()` | ❌ No | Main thread only |
| `world.spawnEntity()` | ❌ No | Main thread only |
| `block.setType()` | ❌ No | Main thread only |
| `inventory.addItem()` | ❌ No | Main thread only |
| `player.getLocation()` | ⚠️ Risky | Snapshot can change |
| Database queries | ✅ Yes | Should be async |
| File I/O | ✅ Yes | Should be async |
| HTTP requests | ✅ Yes | Should be async |

### Prevention Strategy
**Prompt Engineering:**
```
"Use async for database/file/network operations, but switch back to main thread 
(Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask()) before calling Bukkit API. 
Only player.sendMessage() is thread-safe."
```

**Code Review Checklist:**
- [ ] Database queries in `runTaskAsynchronously()`
- [ ] Bukkit API calls in `runTask()` or entity scheduler
- [ ] Async → Sync pattern for combined operations
- [ ] No world modification in async threads
- [ ] No entity spawning in async threads
- [ ] No inventory modification in async threads

**Architecture Prevention:**
Create async utility wrapper:
```java
public final class AsyncUtil {
    
    public static void asyncThenSync(JavaPlugin plugin, 
                                    Runnable asyncTask, 
                                    Runnable syncCallback) {
        Bukkit.getScheduler().runTaskAsynchronously(plugin, () -> {
            asyncTask.run();
            Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask(plugin, syncCallback);
        });
    }
    
    public static <T> CompletableFuture<T> supplyAsync(Supplier<T> supplier, JavaPlugin plugin) {
        return CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(supplier)
            .whenCompleteAsync((result, error) -> {
                // Callback runs on main thread
            }, runnable -> Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask(plugin, runnable));
    }
}
```

# Appendices

## Appendix A: Error Message Decoder

### Quick Reference: Error → Cause → Fix

| Error Message Fragment | Likely Cause | Quick Fix |
|------------------------|--------------|-----------|
| `cannot find symbol: method` | Deprecated/wrong API | Check Paper docs, update method |
| `package does not exist` | Missing dependency | Add Paper to `pom.xml` |
| `class, interface, or enum expected` | YAML in wrong folder | Move `plugin.yml` to `resources/` |
| `illegal start of expression` | Syntax error | Check switch/lambda syntax |
| `method does not override` | Wrong method signature | Remove `@Override` or fix signature |
| `incompatible types` | Wrong type/missing cast | Use type-safe getters |
| `non-static variable` | Static access to instance | Use `getInstance()` or inject dependencies |
| `generic array creation` | `new T[]` not allowed | Use `ArrayList<T>` instead |
| `unchecked cast` | Cast without validation | Add `instanceof` check |
| `has been deprecated` | Old API usage | Use Adventure Components |
| `NullPointerException` | Missing null check | Add null checks/defaults |
| `ConcurrentModificationException` | Modify during iteration | Use `Iterator.remove()` or copy |
| `ClassCastException` | Wrong type cast | Add `instanceof` check |
| `IllegalStateException: Asynchronous` | Bukkit API on async thread | Wrap in `runTask()` |
| `StackOverflowError` | Infinite recursion | Check event triggering events |
| `OutOfMemoryError` | Memory leak/huge load | Paginate queries, fix leaks |
| `NoClassDefFoundError` | Missing dependency | Check dependencies, shade if needed |
| `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` | No args length check | Check `args.length` before access |
| `NumberFormatException` | Invalid number parse | Use try-catch on `parseInt()` |
| `SQLException` | Database error | Check connection, query syntax |

---

## Appendix B: Prevention Cheat Sheet

### 10 Prompt Engineering Phrases to Prevent Errors

```
1. "Use Paper 1.21.4 API with Adventure Components. Never use ChatColor or deprecated methods."

2. "Add null checks for all config values (with defaults), player lookups, ItemStack/ItemMeta, 
   and database queries. Use @NotNull/@Nullable annotations."

3. "Use async (runTaskAsynchronously) for database/file/network operations, 
   then switch to main thread (runTask) before calling Bukkit API."

4. "Always validate with instanceof before casting entities. Use Java 16+ pattern matching."

5. "Use Iterator.remove() or removeIf() to modify collections during iteration. 
   Use ConcurrentHashMap for thread-safe maps."

6. "Check args.length before accessing command arguments. Wrap Integer.parseInt() 
   in try-catch with error messages."

7. "Place plugin.yml in src/main/resources/, use correct YAML syntax with colons, 
   enable Maven resource filtering."

8. "Add Paper dependency to pom.xml with <scope>provided</scope> and correct repository."

9. "Use dependency injection (pass managers through constructors) instead of singleton pattern 
   where possible. Only use getInstance() for cross-plugin API."

10. "Use PreparedStatement with placeholders (?) for all SQL queries. Never concatenate 
    user input into SQL strings."
```

### Architecture Checklist for New Plugins

```markdown
## Project Structure
- [ ] plugin.yml in src/main/resources/
- [ ] Separate packages: commands/, listeners/, managers/, utils/
- [ ] Main class only has onEnable/onDisable
- [ ] Manager pattern for config, database, features

## Dependencies
- [ ] Paper dependency with provided scope
- [ ] Correct Paper repository
- [ ] Resource filtering enabled
- [ ] Version in plugin.yml: ${project.version}

## Code Quality
- [ ] All methods use @NotNull/@Nullable
- [ ] Adventure Components (no ChatColor)
- [ ] Null checks on all external data
- [ ] instanceof before entity casts
- [ ] PreparedStatement for SQL

## Thread Safety
- [ ] Database queries in async
- [ ] Bukkit API in sync
- [ ] ConcurrentHashMap for shared data
- [ ] No collection modification during iteration

## Commands
- [ ] args.length validation
- [ ] Try-catch on parseInt/parseDouble
- [ ] Permission checks
- [ ] Tab completion

## Configuration
- [ ] Default values for all config.get()
- [ ] Validation on reload
- [ ] Type-safe getters (getString, getInt)
```

---

## Appendix C: Debug Flowchart

### Compilation Error Decision Tree

```
Is it a compilation error?
├─ Yes: Does it contain "cannot find symbol"?
│  ├─ Yes: Check Paper dependency in pom.xml
│  │      Check method exists in Paper 1.21.4 docs
│  │      Check for deprecated methods
│  └─ No: Does it contain "class, interface expected"?
│     ├─ Yes: Move plugin.yml to src/main/resources/
│     │      Check YAML syntax (colons, indentation)
│     └─ No: Does it contain "method does not override"?
│        ├─ Yes: Check @EventHandler annotation
│        │      Verify method signature matches event
│        └─ No: Check error message in Appendix A
│
└─ No: Is it a runtime error?
   ├─ Yes: Does it contain "NullPointerException"?
   │  ├─ Yes: Add null checks
   │  │      Add default config values
   │  │      Check player lookups
   │  └─ No: Does it contain "ConcurrentModificationException"?
   │     ├─ Yes: Use Iterator.remove()
   │     │      Create collection copy
   │     └─ No: Does it contain "IllegalStateException: Async"?
   │        ├─ Yes: Wrap Bukkit API in runTask()
   │        │      Move to main thread
   │        └─ No: Check stack trace in Appendix A
   │
   └─ No: Is it a logic bug (compiles but broken)?
      ├─ Yes: Are permissions inverted?
      │  └─ Check !hasPermission vs hasPermission
      ├─ Does event still execute when cancelled?
      │  └─ Add ignoreCancelled = true
      └─ Is data not saving?
         └─ Check async save completion
            Check PlayerQuitEvent priority
```

2. Jump to the relevant section
3. Compare your code with "Bad Code" examples
4. Implement the "Good Code" fix
5. Review the prevention checklist

### For Team Leads
1. Print **Appendix B** and pin next to monitors
2. Add prevention phrases to your AI tool's system prompt
3. Use architecture checklist for code reviews
4. Track common errors in your team to identify training needs

Follow the prevention strategies and good code patterns shown."
